articles.title: Govt won’t roll back new grain procurement norms for paddy: Haryana minister
articles.description: Rana, while speaking to the media, defended the mandi rules and said that the biometric system started this season ensured transparency in issuing gate passes of farmers only bringing crops to the purchase centre.

articles.title: India-Canada eye $50 bn trade as CEPA talks go
articles.description: India and Canada are working to finalize a trade agreement this year. Both nations aim to significantly boost economic cooperation. Bilateral trade is targeted to reach fifty billion dollars by twenty thirty. This renewed engagement follows recent high-level visits. Discussions also cover energy, agriculture, and technology partnerships. A large Indian business delegation is participating in the ongoing talks.

articles.description: Foreign portfolio investors have been fleeing Indian markets, driven by higher capital gains taxes, a weaker rupee, and shifting global allocations.

articles.title: Dividend alert! Last date to buy shares of these 12 companies for dividends worth over Rs 250. Do you own any?
articles.description: Twelve companies are set to turn ex-dividend on Friday, with payouts totaling over Rs 250. Investors must own shares by Thursday to be eligible due to SEBI's T+1 settlement and a market holiday. Bajaj Auto leads with a Rs 150 final dividend.

articles.title: RBI panel to evaluate impact of quantum tech in finance
articles.description: The Reserve Bank of India has formed an eight-member committee to explore quantum technology's role in finance. Headed by an IIT Madras professor, the panel will assess opportunities, risks, and challenges. It will also review the financial sector's readiness for quantum-related threats. The committee aims to recommend a roadmap for safe adoption of quantum applications in finance within six months.

articles.description: UP CM Yogi Adityanath orders timely Kharif 2026 prep, quality seeds/fertilisers, drought contingency plans, crop diversification, and anti-hoarding steps.

articles.description: Homebuyers in 2026 face a complex affordability landscape. While loan rates are historically low, rising construction costs and a shrinking supply of affordable homes present challenges. Buyers must honestly assess their finances and consider long-term implications for a sustainable purchase.

The new hotels offer amenities for longer stays, targeting corporate professionals and relocating families. PRISM plans to integrate India-built technology into these properties, aiming for operational efficiency and an elevated guest experience.

articles.title: FIIs, retail investors raise stakes in 10 midcaps, but stocks slip up to 30%
articles.description: Foreign Institutional Investors (FIIs) and retail investors increased their stakes in nearly 20 midcap stocks over the March quarter. Despite this accumulation, 10 of the top laggards declined by 10% to 30% in the last six months, reflecting weak market sentiment.

articles.title: ‘Work culture is not hell’: Man who worked in Europe, UK, Canada and US says Bengaluru’s real problem is commute
articles.description: A man claimed Bengaluru’s commute, not work culture, is the real reason many professionals feel exhausted.

articles.title: Big Breaking: CTI Proposes 5% VAT Cap to Cut Fuel Prices by ₹15
articles.description: The Chamber of Trade and Industry (CTI) Chairman Brijesh Goyal has written to Petroleum Minister Hardeep Singh Puri, proposing a uniform 5% VAT on petrol and diesel across all states for the next three months. He claims this could reduce fuel prices by ₹10–₹15 per litre, as fuel is outside GST and state taxes cause major price differences. Meanwhile, the All India Motor Transport Congress has expressed strong concern, stating that the transport sector is suffering from repeated fuel hikes and calling it a “slow poison” for the industry. They argue that instead of frequent small increases, a single large revision would help businesses plan better. Fuel prices have risen for the fourth time in 11 days, with petrol up ₹2.61 and diesel ₹2.71 per litre. Analysts also compared global fuel price trends, noting that while many countries have seen much higher increases over the years, India’s rise is relatively lower at around 7–8%. However, public reactions in cities like Lucknow reflect growing concern over rising daily expenses and uncertainty about future hikes.

articles.title: Torrent Pharma rises 5% on Q4 beat; brokerages divided on JBCP integration
articles.description: Torrent Pharma's revenue from operations rose 42 per cent Y-o-Y to ₹4,197 crore in Q4FY26 from ₹2,959 crore in Q4FY25

articles.title: RBI signals intervention readiness as rupee seen ‘undervalued’ after 6% slide: Report
articles.description: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) has signalled readiness to intervene in the foreign exchange market to ensure orderly currency movements, while Governor Sanjay Malhotra said the rupee appears undervalued after depreciating around 6 per cent since tensions in West Asia escalated

articles.title: INR regains ground as oil prices retreat
articles.description: The Indian rupee regained further ground in opening trades on Monday tracking sharp pullback in international oil prices and weakness in greenback overseas. Improving risk sentiments amid expectation of US and Iran peace deal is waning demand for the safe haven asset. The dollar index is seen trading under 99 mark at 98.98. Washington and Tehran have reportedly signaled progress in talks to end the war, even as US President Donald Trump said he wont rush into an agreement. INR opened at Rs 95.36 per dollar and hit a high of 95.20 so far during the day. Yesterday, the counter settled at 95.60. The BSE Sensex is trading at 76,302.90, surging by 887.55 points (1.18%), and the NSE Nifty 50 is at 23,962.35, climbing 243.05 points (1.02%) as of mid-morning. The markets rallied sharply following eased geopolitical tensions and dropping crude oil prices. .

articles.title: India Pesticides surges after reporting strong Q4 results
articles.description: India Pesticides jumped 6.63% to Rs 176.15 after the company reported a strong performance for the fourth quarter of FY26, with consolidated net profit rising 43.70% to Rs 31.04 crore, compared with Rs 21.60 crore in Q4 FY25.

articles.title: Nykaa Q4 PAT surges over four-fold to Rs 78 cr
articles.description: FSN E-commerce Ventures (Nykaa) reported a 313.39% jump in consolidated net profit to Rs 78.75 crore on 28.44% increase in revenue from operations to Rs 2,648.17 crore in Q4 FY26 over Q4 FY25.

articles.title: Salzer Electronics drops after Q4 PAT slumps 17% QoQ to Rs 10.26 cr
articles.description: Salzer Electronics declined 5.50% to Rs 666.20 after the company's consolidated net profit tumbled 17.12% to Rs 10.26 crore despite 11.77% jump in revenue from operations to Rs 474.14 crore in Q4 FY26 over Q3 FY26.

articles.title: Universal Cables soars after Q4 profit rises 103% QoQ
articles.description: Shares of Universal Cables surged 9.68% to Rs 1,111.80 after the company reported strong quarterly earnings and announced fresh investment plans for capacity modernization.
